peer_exemplars.reasoning: High conscientiousness and low libertarianism drive skepticism of mandated transparency as intrusive regulation, while high openness allows some nuance.

Extreme libertarian anti-censorship (96.4) and free-spirit autonomy (96.27) drive strong support for transparency, while low trust in institutions (35.12) and a dismissive-avoidant attachment reinforce a desire to see how algorithms shape information.
High libertarianism and anti-censorship orientation drive strong support for transparency, but low trust in institutions tempers it slightly.
Moderate support for algorithmic transparency, driven by high dangerous-worldview and anti-domination values, tempered by low openness and a preference for order.
